data.title: Diamond Number Pyramid Pattern
data.statement:
<<Goal>> : 
Given an integer [[n]], output a symmetric diamond-shaped digit pattern.
For a diamond of size 2 × [[n]] − 1, the rules are:

Each row [[i]] ([[i]] = 1 to [[n]]) starts from the integer [[i]].
Digits in each row increase step by step toward the center and then mirror back.
The middle row is the widest row containing (2 × [[n]] − 1) digits with no leading spaces.
All other rows are centered by adding spaces on the left.
The bottom half of the diamond is a vertically symmetric copy of the top half (excluding the middle row).
data.outputDescription: <<2 × [[n]] − 1 lines>>: The symmetric diamond pattern of digits for [[n]].

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Goal :
Given an integer n, output a symmetric diamond-shaped digit pattern.
For a diamond of size 2 × n − 1, the rules are:

Each row i (i = 1 to n) starts from the integer i.
Digits in each row increase step by step toward the center and then mirror back.
The middle row is the widest row containing (2 × n − 1) digits with no leading spaces.
All other rows are centered by adding spaces on the left.
The bottom half of the diamond is a vertically symmetric copy of the top half (excluding the middle row).
Input
A single integer n.
Output
2 × n − 1 lines: The symmetric diamond pattern of digits for n.
Constraints
1 ≤ n ≤ 5
Example
Input
3
Output
  1
 232
34543
 232
  1
